test.detail: 567 tests passed across 20 test files, 0 failures

lint.detail: 69 warnings (31 dead_code + 38 clippy style suggestions), all pre-existing, no errors

nonBlockingIssues.description: LOCK-ORDERING.md level numbering inconsistency: states 'level N may only acquire level N+1+' but KERNEL_PT_LOCK (level 3) acquires page_allocator (level 2) and slab (level 2) acquires page_allocator (level 2). Correctly explained in prose but table contradicts stated rule.
Missing blank line before remove_empty_process() doc comment in scheduler.rs — minor formatting inconsistency.
Test process_slot_leak_on_thread_alloc_failure asserts the bug condition and applies fix inline rather than strict failure-first TDD. Acceptable for cross-module verification tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify TDD expectations for cross-file invariant audits and documentation-only features. The current TDD step requires 'write a test that FAILS before the fix', but cross-module invariant analysis often finds 'verified safe' results (4 of 5 invariants in this case). The skill's Special Cases section for cross-file features should explicitly state: (a) no new tests are expected when no bugs are found, (b) verification tests that confirm correct behavior (rather than demonstrating failure-first) are acceptable for cross-module audits.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both lock-ordering-audit (documentation-only, no bugs found → no tests needed) and cross-module-lifetime-audit (4/5 invariants verified safe, 1 bug found and fixed) workers noted this ambiguity in their skillFeedback.suggestedChanges. Two independent workers flagged the same issue.

rejectedObservations.observation: No services.yaml file exists in the repo (.factory/services.yaml). The AGENTS.md references it for build/test/stress commands but the file is missing.
rejectedObservations.reason: Incorrect — .factory/services.yaml EXISTS and was successfully read during this validation. The reviewer's glob search may have been misconfigured.
